item.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

item.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

item.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

item.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

item.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
item.dc:creator: Cindy Zackney
item.description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

item.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

item.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

item.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

item.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

item.title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, Sean O'Kane
item.description: A new era is on the way for Apple as Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus.   Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosystem than the one Cook spent decades shaping. The App […]

item.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

item.title: Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga joins stacked StrictlyVC SF lineup for April 30 event
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Surprise! StrictlyVC San Francisco, which will kick off this year’s events lineup for TechCrunch on April 30 at the Sentro Filipino Cultural Center, is getting a new addition to its increasingly stacked lineup of speakers. Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga will join the rest of the lineup to discuss operating at scale in the age of AI.

item.title: Tim Cook is stepping down. What happens to Apple now?
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus. Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosystemthan the one Cook spent decades shaping. The App Store’s 30% cut is under pressure, the behind-the-scenes power Apple once […]

item.title: Nothing introduces an AI-powered dictation tool
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Nothing's new on-device dictation tool supports over 100 languages.

item.title: DeepSeek previews new AI model that ‘closes the gap’ with frontier models
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: DeepSeek says both models are more efficient and performant than DeepSeek V3.2 due to architectural improvements, and have almost "closed the gap" with current leading models, both open and closed, on reasoning benchmarks.

item.title: In another wild turn for AI chips, Meta signs deal for millions of Amazon AI CPUs
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Meta has commandeered a big chunk of Amazon's homegrown CPUs (not GPUs) for AI agentic workloads, signaling that a new kind of chip race has begun.

item.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

item.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.
